desc: Command: Migrates a piping system from another open Revit model.
summary: Command: Migrates a piping system from another open Revit model. This is the main "Migrate System" command that: 1. Opens dialog to select another already-open Revit model 2. Displays available systems in that model 3. On system selection, imports associated routing preference families 4. Imports the system type and configures routing preferences
usecase: User wants to copy a drainage system setup from a template model. User needs to bring in pipe types and fittings from another project. User wants to standardize system configurations across projects.
notfor: Custom UI required for manual system/family browsing. AI path is JSON-first and dialog-free.
precondition: At least two project documents must be open in Revit. Active document must not be read-only. Source model must contain piping systems with routing preferences.
postcondition: Selected system type is created/updated in current document. All associated families are imported (overwriting if needed). Routing preferences are configured with the imported families.
logictree: check: openDocuments.count > 1 → continuecheck: sourceDoc != targetDoc → continuecheck: sourceSystems.count > 0 → showSystemListcheck: selectedSystem.hasRoutingPreferences → importRoutingPreferencesresolve: transaction.success → showResults

preprompts: Preparing to migrate piping system from another model...
Setting up system import from source model...
thinkingsteps: Scanning open documents for source models...
Importing system and configuring routing preferences...
successprompts: Migration complete — routing preferences configured for {ImportedSystem}.
